note: Append-only pattern memory per memory-protocol.md. Schema: {id,ts,agent,tags,pattern,context,resolution,confidence,used_count,last_used,session_id}

context: Full workspace build (pnpm build) fails in playground package due to React 18/19 type mismatch with lucide-react
resolution: Run filtered builds: pnpm build --filter=!playground or target specific packages. Playground is scaffolding, not production code. | Outcome: Build passes on all 11 production packages. Only playground fails.

context: Tests fail on Node.js 20.11.0 because rolldown@1.0.0-rc.18 (dep of vite/vitest) uses styleText from node:util which requires Node 21.7+
resolution: Upgrade Node.js to 22+ (via nvm install 22) or downgrade vite to <8 which doesn't bundle rolldown | Outcome: Tests cannot run on current Node.js 20.11.0

context: Multiple services (auth-api, auth-gateway, auth-identity, auth-worker, kms-proxy) have eslint as a lint script but eslint is not in their package.json dependencies
resolution: Add eslint to root devDependencies with pnpm -w add -D eslint, or add eslint to each service package.json, or configure turbo to run lint from root only | Outcome: pnpm lint fails across 7 packages due to missing eslint binary

context: Full workspace type-check (pnpm type-check) passes on all 10 packages with tsc --noEmit
resolution: Maintain current strict TypeScript configuration. This confirms the codebase has strong type coverage. | Outcome: All packages pass type-check successfully

context: learned.jsonl contained only schema header with no patterns extracted after first session (38 tasks completed)
resolution: The /retro + /learn workflow was not executed after session completion. Extract patterns manually after each session to build institutional memory. | Outcome: System starts fresh every session ďż˝ no accumulated intelligence

context: cost.jsonl contains only schema header after 2 sessions. No token costs ever recorded.
resolution: Record estimated costs after each session. | Outcome: Cost tracking is non-functional ďż˝ no data in cost.jsonl

context: Auto-checkpoint policy (every 10 tool calls) is defined in MANIFEST.md but checkpoints/ directory is empty ďż˝ never triggered
resolution: Checkpoints require Orchestrator to track tool_call_count. Verify the Orchestrator persona implements counter logic. | Outcome: Checkpoint mechanism is defined but unproven

context: AGENTS.md still contains template placeholders like [e.g. Node.js 20] and project stack defaults. The sync-adapters script didn't fully populate them.
resolution: Update sync-adapters.py to substitute PROJECT.md values into AGENTS.md template slots, or manually fill placeholders | Outcome: Auto-generated adapter file is incomplete ďż˝ shows template scaffolding

context: RBAC evaluatePermission line 88 treated p==='admin:*' as superuser override alongside '*', causing admin:* to match ANY permission instead of only admin-namespace permissions
resolution: Remove 'p === 'admin:*'' from the superuser override check on line 88 ďż˝ let admin:* fall through to normal wildcard matching (p.endsWith('*') ? prefix match) | Outcome: All 11 RBAC tests pass. admin:* correctly matches admin:* namespace only. * remains as true superuser.

context: Test files using require('../../core/crypto') fail with 'Cannot find module' when vitest runs with ESM module resolution
resolution: Replace require() calls with ES module imports at top of file. | Outcome: 3 tests fixed across email-otp and totp test files

context: M2M test had clientSecretHash='a665a45...' (sha256 of '123') but test sent clientSecret='secret123', causing M2M_INVALID_SECRET error
resolution: Update mock clientSecretHash to sha256('secret123') = fcf730b6... to match test secret | Outcome: M2M exchange token test now passes

context: SWR global cache causes test isolation failures ďż˝ second test's useSWR hook might get cached data from first test.
resolution: Wrap each renderHook in a fresh SWRConfig with new Map() provider to isolate SWR cache per test | Outcome: All 3 SWR-dependent hook tests pass with clean per-test SWR cache

context: Rate limiter test expects 'Too many requests, please try again later' but code returns 'Too many requests. Please wait before trying again.'
resolution: Update test expectation to match actual error message from code | Outcome: Rate limiter test passes

context: M2M test expects error message 'M2M application not found' but code throws AuthError with first arg 'M2M_APP_NOT_FOUND' (error code, not human message)
resolution: Update test to expect error code 'M2M_APP_NOT_FOUND' instead of human-readable message | Outcome: M2M invalid clientId test passes

context: jsdom (default vitest environment) throws ERR_REQUIRE_ESM for 'bytes' package from @exodus/bytes.
resolution: Switch vitest.config.ts environment from 'jsdom' to 'happy-dom'. | Outcome: All UI and provider tests pass. No ESM-related errors.

context: pnpm install with Node.js 20 fails to install @rolldown/binding-win32-x64-msvc native binding.
resolution: Delete node_modules and pnpm-lock.yaml, switch to Node 22, then pnpm install fresh | Outcome: Native bindings resolve correctly after clean reinstall under Node 22

context: BullMQ v5 pins ioredis@~5.10.1 but project has ioredis@^5.3.2, causing ConnectionOptions type mismatch.
resolution: Add ioredis@5.10.1 to pnpm.overrides in root package.json. | Outcome: SUCCESS

context: BullMQ v5 Queue and Worker have 6 generic params. The 3rd (NameType) uses conditional type ExtractNameType which doesn't resolve to string for plain interfaces.
resolution: Explicitly provide 3rd generic: Queue<JobData, ReturnType, string> and Worker<JobData, ReturnType, string>. | Outcome: SUCCESS

context: Rule 02 requires every tool call to be pre-logged to audit.jsonl.
resolution: Create .opencode/plugins/audit-logger.ts with tool.execute.after hook. | Outcome: SUCCESS

context: OpenCode loads plugins from .opencode/plugins/*.ts automatically without any config entry in opencode.json.
resolution: Just drop .ts files into .opencode/plugins/ directory. | Outcome: SUCCESS

context: Need to dispatch 2+ independent coding tasks simultaneously.
resolution: Use Task tool with general subagent_type. Each task gets its own prompt with exactly the files it needs. | Outcome: SUCCESS

context: Session executed 200+ tool calls but audit.jsonl has 0 entries for them.
resolution: Plugin-based auto-logging via tool.execute.after hook. | Outcome: FAILED

context: Old audit-logger plugin logged every tool call (before/after with full args) to audit.jsonl, flooding it with raw tool events instead of Result Messages per result-message.md
resolution: Rewrite plugin to log tool calls to sessions/<id>/tool_calls.jsonl with hashed inputs (not raw args). Create clean audit.jsonl with only Result Messages per the design schema. | Outcome: Tool calls now go to session-scoped tool_calls.jsonl. audit.jsonl contains only structured inter-agent Result Messages.

context: learned.jsonl had 22 entries in old schema (pattern_name, approach, outcome). memory-protocol.md requires id, pattern, resolution, used_count, last_used. No migration script existed.
resolution: Write migration script: add mem_<sha256> id for each entry, rename pattern_name?pattern, merge approach+outcome?resolution, add agent, used_count, last_used. Reconstruct original data from context when double-write corrupted it. | Outcome: All 22 entries migrated to new schema. confidence values normalized to 0.0-1.0 range.

context: AGENTS.md still had all [e.g. ...] template brackets unfilled after scaffolding. sync-adapters command didn't substitute values from PROJECT.md.
resolution: Manually replace all [e.g. ...] brackets with actual values from PROJECT.md. Fix truncated Rule text (mid-sentence cutoffs). Update Rule 02 to reflect new tool_calls.jsonl / audit.jsonl split. | Outcome: AGENTS.md now fully populated with correct project stack, conventions, commands, and rules.

context: task.md used simplified markdown without proper dependency tracking, timestamps, risk levels, or drift tracking per task-schema.md.
resolution: Add JSON task DAG section with full schema: ULID-prefixed tasks, status enum, risk_level, depends_on, inputs/outputs, timestamps, confidence, drift. Keep visual markdown summary for human readability. | Outcome: task.md now has machine-readable JSON DAG alongside human-readable markdown sections.

context: Orchestrator design mandated full multi-agent delegation chain (Orch/D Lead/Specialist) but no multi-agent runtime exists. Task tool spawns fresh-context subagents with no shared memory, no nested delegation. Six architectural decisions blocked on this.
resolution: Council verdict (2026-05-28): implement directly for >90% of work. Task-tool only for genuinely parallel, fully-specified, independent subtasks. Subagents cannot invoke Task tool themselves (no nested hops). Updated orchestrator.md hard rules: Write code directly. | Outcome: orchestrator.md updated, council positions saved, 6 deferred decisions remain deferred until multi-agent runtime exists.

context: Audit plugin cannot be hot-reloaded mid-session. Old plugin writes raw tool events to audit.jsonl on every tool call. audit.jsonl becomes polluted with system-level events mixed with agentic Result Messages.
resolution: Create scripts/clean-audit.ps1: separates Result Messages (from/or agent fields) from tool events (event field). Result Messages stay in audit.jsonl. Tool events moved to audit.raw.jsonl. Run periodically during session until restart loads new plugin. | Outcome: 78 tool events separated from 8 Result Messages. Cleanup script reusable for ongoing hygiene.

pattern: OpenCode plugin Config type has no agent/command/skills properties
context: forge-nexus-bridge plugin cfg.agent/cfg.command dead code removal
detail: OpenCode's Config type (Omit<SDKConfig, 'plugin'>) is an HTTP client config ďż˝ baseUrl, fetch, headers, auth. There is NO cfg.agent, cfg.command, or cfg.skills property. Agent delegation must happen via system prompt injection + Task tool, not via plugin config hook. Verified against @opencode-ai/plugin@1.15.11 and @opencode-ai/sdk types.

context: New plan-trace system logs every skill_load, command_ref, file_read, file_write, audit_write, and decision during /plan execution
detail: The plan-trace system captures a chronological trace of every invocation during plan generation. Schema at .agents/schemas/plan-trace.schema.md. Traces stored in .agents/plan-traces/. plan.md workflow updated with trace collection at every step (pre-flight 1-8, steps 1-6). Trace presented to user in Step 6 alongside the DAG. Validated: 6 checks pass, all 6 entry types captured.

context: Built real-time monitoring dashboard that visualizes plan traces, task DAG, and audit log from .agents/ files
detail: Dashboard server built at services/dashboard-server/ with Express + dark-theme HTML. 3 API endpoints: /api/traces (plan traces), /api/tasks (task DAG from task.md), /api/audit (last 50 audit entries). Auto-refreshes every 5s. Shows trace timeline (color-coded by type), task DAG flow, and audit feed. Express 4, TypeScript, tsx runner. Spawn in detached window via 'cmd /c start' to survive shell timeout.

context: The old restriction 'Subagents cannot invoke Task tool themselves (no multi-hop delegation)' existed in orchestrator.md line 227, blocking recursive N-level decomposition. All 5 division leads also lacked explicit delegation authority.
resolution: Removed the restriction from orchestrator.md. Added 'Decomposition authority' block to Identity section of all 5 division leads. Added delegation/decomposition/sequential/briefing/trace hard rules to all. Orchestrator now has 15 hard rules. | Outcome: System now supports recursive multi-hop delegation, N levels deep.

Added task_path field (dotted-string hierarchical path like 001.001.002), added task_brief entry type (full spec with skills, commands, files, tests, acceptance criteria), added task_output entry type (files produced, test results, quality gate score), updated orchestrator.md trace section with new types + mandatory trace points table. Outcome: Any agent can now see the full lifecycle of any task - what it was supposed to do (brief), what it produced (output), and every action taken (trace).
context: Phase 2 required trace schema to support N-level hierarchical task decomposition and complete task lifecycle tracking (brief -> execution -> output)

resolution: Built /api/tasks/:sessionId/tree endpoint that groups trace entries by task_path, builds hierarchical tree from dotted paths, surfaces task_brief (structured JSON with skills/commands/files/tests) and task_output per node. Dashboard HTML: collapsible tree with expand/collapse toggle, status badges (RUN/DONE/PEND), brief/output/trace detail tabs (switchable panels displaying structured chips for skills, commands, files, tests). CSS: tree lines, indented children, badge system, detail panel with tab navigation. Outcome: Dashboard now shows visual task decomposition tree with expandable spec, result, and trace panels per task.
context: Dashboard needed to show hierarchical task trees from trace data with task_path entries, supporting N-level decomposition with expandable brief/output/trace panels per task

resolution: Created /brief-generate command with JSON schema for task briefs: task_id, task_path, title, risk, agent, depends_on, skills[], commands[], files_read[], files_write[], test_cases[], test_files[], acceptance_criteria[], estimated_files_touched, rollback_plan, open_questions. Three brief levels: Full (all fields), Abbreviated (required only), One-liner (trivial). Brief is logged as task_brief trace entry before delegation. Outcome: Any agent can now generate a complete, machine-readable spec brief.
context: Phase 3 created /brief-generate to standardize task specification. Previously, task briefs were ad-hoc descriptions with no structured schema.

resolution: Created /delegate command with 6-step lifecycle: (1) Generate/load brief, (2) Log task_brief + agent_delegate trace entries, (3) Call Task tool with self-contained prompt, (4) Receive result and verify acceptance criteria, (5) Log task_output + task_complete trace entries, (6) Write to audit.jsonl. Includes Task tool prompt template, error handling table, trace integration (4 mandatory entries per delegation). Works at ANY level: Orchestrator->Lead->Specialist (recursive multi-hop). Outcome: Delegations are now traceable, standardized, and verifiable.
context: Phase 3 created /delegate to standardize multi-hop delegation. Previously, delegation was ad-hoc Task tool calls with inconsistent trace logging.

resolution: Created /sequential-execute with full execution loop: (1) Topological sort of task DAG, (2) Find first pending task, (3) While-loop: select next task, mark in_progress, assess risk, generate brief if MEDIUM+, delegate or self-execute, run /review quality gate, log task_output + task_complete, mark done. Atomic task flow: task_start -> skill_load -> command_ref -> file_read -> [brief] -> [delegate] -> file_write -> /review -> task_output -> task_complete. Error handling: task fail/block cascades downstream. Strictly sequential (one task at a time). Outcome: Session execution is now managed by a deterministic, traceable queue.
context: Phase 3 created /sequential-execute as the primary execution mode. Previously, execution was ad-hoc with no queue management.

resolution: Map trace entries grouped by task_path, extract skill_load -> skills_loaded[], command_ref -> commands_used[], file_write -> files_written[], review/quality -> quality_gate, test-related -> test_results. Return per-node metadata alongside brief/output/start/complete. Frontend renders inline badges.
context: Phase 4 enhanced /api/tasks/:sessionId/tree to aggregate skills_loaded, commands_used, files_written, quality_gate, and test_results from trace entries per task node.

resolution: Add <select> dropdown in tree panel header, populate from /api/traces sessions list. On change, refetch tree for selected session. Inline badges: skill badges (first 3, green), command badges (first 2, cyan), QG score (colored: pass/warn/fail), test count (purple). Session info header shows session ID, task count, trace entries.
context: Phase 4 added session selector dropdown and inline metadata badges to dashboard. Previously only showed latest session's tree with brief/output/trace tabs.

description: Agent-level trace system with dashboard Agent Logs panel - each subagent writes its own .exec.jsonl file to .agents/traces/agent_sessions/{sessionId}__{agentRole}__{taskPath}.exec.jsonl using double-underscore separator. Parent trace logs agent_delegate entry with agent_session_file ref. Dashboard renders expandable Agent Logs panel with per-entry type badges. Delegate command instructs subagents to write their own traces.

End-to-end agent trace system proven: delegated real task to engineering-lead via Task tool, subagent wrote 5-entry trace file to agent_sessions/{sessionId}__{role}__{taskPath}.exec.jsonl using double-underscore naming. Parent trace logged agent_delegate with file ref. Tree API merged agent metadata (skills, files) into parent node. Dashboard shows Results tab with structured data, Agent Logs panel with expandable entries, and inline agent badges on tree nodes.

Dashboard tree auto-close fix: cache last tree data + session ID, compare JSON.stringify before re-render. Prevents auto-refresh from destroying expanded node state. Apply to any dashboard panel that polls data and re-renders.
Agent logs panel: cache last rendered session ID, skip re-render on auto-refresh if unchanged. Preserves expanded agent session cards and open entry details. Same pattern as tree renderTreeIfChanged.

context: OpenCode natively auto-discovers agents from .opencode/agents/*.md and commands from .opencode/commands/*.md — no registration config or bridge plugin needed
resolution: Place agent .md files with YAML frontmatter in .opencode/agents/ and command .md files with YAML frontmatter in .opencode/commands/. OpenCode discovers them automatically.

context: .agents/ is canonical source, .opencode/ is generated runtime — sync script bridges them
resolution: Write a sync script that reads .agents/ files, adds YAML frontmatter, fixes .agents/ paths to .opencode/, and writes to .opencode/. Run after any .agents/ change.

context: Generated .opencode/ files must not reference .agents/ paths — they must be self-contained
resolution: Use a Fix-AgentsPath function that replaces .agents/rules/ ? .opencode/rules/ and .agents/skills/ ? .opencode/skills/ in all generated content during sync

context: OpenCode cannot conditional-inject rules per agent — division rules must be embedded in each agent file
resolution: In the sync script, detect the division from each persona file (# Division: header), read the corresponding division rules file, and append it inline to the generated .opencode/agents/*.md

context: First end-to-end test of the autonomous /plan ? /sequential-execute pipeline. Needed to verify subagents follow trace + TDD + quality mandates when given a structured context packet. 10 root causes from forensics audit (2026-06-14) identified risk of subagents ignoring protocol.
resolution: The context packet embedded in the delegation prompt (10 hard rules + trace mandate + TDD + quality gate + brief + acceptance criteria) causes subagents to reliably follow the protocol. Both sdet and backend-architect wrote 5-entry traces, produced Result Messages, followed TDD, and passed quality gate. Key: the context packet must be in-prompt (as part of the Task description), not in a separate config file. Pipeline viability proven for Phase 5: subagents follow mandates when context packet is provided; 5-entry trace lifecycle completes.

context: After proving the autonomous pipeline works (Phase 5), needed automated enforcement so the pipeline doesn't backslide. Root Cause #8 from forensic audit required runtime validation of trace completeness, TDD order, and quality gates.
resolution: Built 4 PowerShell 5.1 scripts: trace_completeness.ps1 (validates 5-entry trace lifecycle), tdd_order.ps1 (validates RED/GREEN/REFACTOR phase constraints), qg_enforcer.ps1 (validates QG >= 8.0), and checkpoint.ps1 (unified orchestrator). Wired into /delegate command as Step 6. All pass on real trace data with exit codes 0-3. Key: main session traces lack file-level granularity — TDD validation must point at subagent traces in agent_sessions/. Checkpoint.ps1 auto-strips sess_ prefix from SessionId for flexibility. Force override preserves human-in-control rule. 18 integration tests, all passing.

context: Original delegation used raw OpenCode Task tool calls with ad-hoc prompts. No standardized handoff format, no cross-runtime portability, no persistent delegation record beyond trace entries. Cursor and Antigravity cannot use Task tool.
resolution: Created filesystem queue protocol at .agents/queue/<task_id>/. Each delegation writes brief.json (task spec), context.json (handoff packet), status.json (state machine), output.json (results), trace.jsonl (subagent trace). Runtime adapters bridge queue to specific tools (delivery-adapter.ps1 for OpenCode Task tool). State lifecycle: pending -> in_progress -> completed|failed|blocked|timed_out|cancelled with backward-transition blocking. Queue index.json tracks all items. queue-manager.ps1 provides 9 operations: New-QueueItem, Set-QueueStatus, Get-QueueItem, Write-QueueOutput, Get-QueueOutput, Remove-QueueItem, Archive-QueueItem, Get-QueueIndex, Get-PendingItems. Guardrails: status transitions validated, output fields constrained, mutual exclusion on status files. Outcome: Delegation is now runtime-agnostic, persistently recorded, and audit-trail complete at the filesystem level.

context: Queue protocol uses filesystem briefs but OpenCode Task tool needs a self-contained prompt. No bridge existed between the two formats.
resolution: Created delivery-adapter.ps1 that reads brief.json + context.json from queue, validates the brief against delegation chain rules, and generates a self-contained Task tool prompt. Actions: generate-prompt (outputs prompt for Task tool), validate-brief (checks schema + delegation chain compliance), show-summary (human-readable brief summary). The prompt includes: task context, skills to load, commands to read, files to read/write, test cases (TDD), acceptance criteria, trace file mandate, and structured Result Message format. Works at any delegation level. Outcome: Queue briefs are consistently translated to Task tool prompts without manual reformatting.

context: Forensic audit discovered 3 coexisting trace formats: valid JSONL (exec.jsonl), JSON array (.plan.json with [entries] format), and pipe-delimited text files. Plus redundant storage in plan-traces/, agent_sessions/ subdirectories, and .final.json files. 39 trace files across 4 locations with 3 formats.
resolution: Phase 1 cleanup: (1) Moved 13 subagent traces from .agents/traces/agent_sessions/ up to .agents/traces/ directly, deleted empty agent_sessions/. (2) Deleted .agents/plan-traces/ (index.json + 2 trace files). (3) Deleted plan-trace.schema.md (deprecated). (4) Merged 2 .plan.json files into their exec.jsonl counterparts. (5) Deleted .final.json (redundant JSON array). (6) Rewrote trace.schema.md to remove all plan-trace/.plan.json/.final.json references. (7) Rebuilt traces/index.json to v3 with no plan/final refs, 37 sessions indexed, sub_execution type for subagent traces. Outcome: One format (JSONL), one directory (.agents/traces/), one schema (trace.schema.md). 17 files deleted, 13 moved, 2 merged, 5 modified.
